notes: Silver DQ rules for Gemma scores — post-SOC-normalization, post-error-filter. Covers schema validity, distribution sanity, and referential integrity to O*NET.

rules.name: SOC format XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code_normalized IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(soc_code_normalized, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: All normalized SOC codes must match XX-XXXX. Silver rejects un-normalizable inputs; this rule catches regressions in the normalizer.

rules.name: Exposure score range 0-10
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score < 0 OR exposure_score > 10
rules.description: Scorer validates 0-10 before returning; this catches any drift in that validator.

rules.name: No mode collapse (<=40% any single score)
rules.sql: WITH score_counts AS (SELECT exposure_score, COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure), 0) AS pct FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure GROUP BY exposure_score) SELECT * FROM score_counts WHERE pct > 40
rules.description: A single exposure_score claiming >40% of the population is a mode-collapse signal — Gemma is returning a default value.
rules.rationale: v4 spec §4 Silver DQ — mode-collapse guardrail. Catches the 'all 7s' failure mode explicit in v3 review.

rules.name: Task breakdown arrays are valid JSON
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure WHERE (task_breakdown_automatable IS NOT NULL AND TRY_CAST(task_breakdown_automatable AS JSON) IS NULL) OR (task_breakdown_human IS NOT NULL AND TRY_CAST(task_breakdown_human AS JSON) IS NULL)
rules.description: Task breakdowns are stored as JSON strings. Invalid JSON breaks the MCP _JSON_STRUCT_FIELDS decode path.

rules.name: Referential integrity: SOC exists in onet_work_profiles
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure WHERE NOT join_valid
rules.description: Every Gemma SOC must exist in consumable.onet_work_profiles. join_valid is computed at transform time; failures here indicate a mismatch between scorer input and current O*NET Gold.

rules.name: Bucket coverage: >=10% each in {0-3, 4-6, 7-10}
rules.sql: WITH buckets AS (SELECT CASE WHEN exposure_score BETWEEN 0 AND 3 THEN 'low' WHEN exposure_score BETWEEN 4 AND 6 THEN 'mid' WHEN exposure_score BETWEEN 7 AND 10 THEN 'high' END AS bucket FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure), bucket_pct AS (SELECT bucket, COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure), 0) AS pct FROM buckets GROUP BY bucket) SELECT * FROM bucket_pct WHERE pct < 10
rules.description: Each of the three buckets (low / mid / high exposure) must hold at least 10% of scored occupations. Guards against bimodal or compressed distributions.
rules.rationale: v4 §4 A/B Gate 7 mirrored at Silver — distribution sanity applies independently of the Karpathy overlap.

rules.name: Standard deviation >= 1.5
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN stddev_pop(exposure_score) >= 1.5 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure
rules.description: Population std dev >= 1.5 catches distributions too narrow to differentiate careers even when no single mode dominates.

rules.name: No duplicate normalized SOCs
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code_normalized, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base_gemma_ai_exposure GROUP BY soc_code_normalized HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: Grain uniqueness — one row per normalized SOC.
